description: Pre-Session B classification for pray (reg 212). First iteration export. No terms extracted yet — all None or candidate_delete. Sets anchor verse. Extracts primary prayer vocabulary (17 terms). Rescues 3 terms from candidate_delete. Confirms 1 extracted_thin. Bulk-deletes bleed vocabulary. Flags 4 missing terms requiring Phase 1 supplement from Claude Code before Session B closure.

operations.description: Set provisional anchor verse Rom 8:26-27 ('the Spirit himself intercedes for us with groanings too deep for words... according to the will of God'). Most comprehensive single statement about prayer as spirit-to-Spirit communication: the believer's weakness, the Spirit's intercession, and the Father's will in one passage. To be confirmed after verse reading. Secondary candidate: Mat 6:6 (pray to your Father in secret) or 1Co 14:14-15 (spirit prays / mind unfruitful).
G4336 proseuchomai → extracted. PRIMARY NT PRAY VERB. 167 occurrences, 81 verses. The most important NT prayer term. Cover

set.status_note: PRIMARY NT PRAY VERB. 167 occurrences, 81 verses. The most important NT prayer term. Covers: the Lord's teaching on prayer (Mat 6:5-9), Gethsemane (Mat 26:36-44), apostolic instruction (Rom 12:12; Eph 6:18; Phi 4:6; Col 4:2; 1Th 5:17), Paul's own prayer practice (Rom 1:10; Phi 1:9), praying in the Spirit (Eph 6:18; Jude 20), and the prayer that raises the sick (Jam 5:14-18). Mat 6:6 — 'when you pr

set.status_note: PRIMARY NT PRAYER NOUN. 103 occurrences, 36 verses. Phi 4:6 — 'in everything by prayer (proseuche) and petition (deēsis) with thanksgiving.' Act 1:14 — 'all these with one accord were devoting themselves to prayer (proseuche).' Eph 6:18 — 'praying at all times in the Spirit, with all prayer (proseuche) and supplication.' The noun form of proseuchomai — covers the act, the content, and the practice

set.status_note: PRIMARY OT PRAY VERB. 84 occurrences, 82 verses. Gen 20:7 — 'he is a prophet, so that he will pray (pa.lal) for you.' 1Sa 1:10 — Hannah praying in bitterness of soul. 1Ki 8:28-54 — Solomon's temple dedication prayer (pa.lal used 9 times). Dan 9:4 — Daniel's great intercessory prayer. The primary Hebrew verb for directed address to God. Covers individual petition, intercession for others, and litur

operations.description: H8605 te.phil.lah → extracted. PRIMARY OT PRAYER NOUN. 77 occurrences, 70 verses. 1Ki 8:28 — 'have regard to the prayer (

set.status_note: PRIMARY OT PRAYER NOUN. 77 occurrences, 70 verses. 1Ki 8:28 — 'have regard to the prayer (te.phil.lah) of your servant.' Psa 72:20 — 'the prayers (te.phi.lot) of David, son of Jesse, are ended.' Hab 3:1 — 'a prayer (te.phil.lah) of Habakkuk.' The primary Hebrew noun for prayer — covers individual petitions, collected prayers, and the great intercessory prayers of the OT. Shares root with pa.lal.

operations.description: H6279 a.tar → extracted. TO ENTREAT/PRAY. 20 occurrences, 19 verses. Gen 25:21 — 'Isaac prayed (a.tar) to the Lord

set.status_note: TO ENTREAT/PRAY. 20 occurrences, 19 verses. Gen 25:21 — 'Isaac prayed (a.tar) to the Lord for his wife, because she was barren.' Exo 8:8-30 — Moses entreating God to remove the plagues (used 4 times). 2Sa 21:14 — 'God was moved by prayer (a.tar) for the land.' The a.tar vocabulary covers urgent petition that moves God to act — particularly concentrated in Exodus and the patriarchal narratives.

Psa 28:2 — 'hear the voice of my pleas (te.chin.nah) for mercy.' Ezr 9:8 — 'grace has been shown from the Lord our God.' Dan 9:3 — 'I turned my face to the Lord God, seeking him by prayer and pleas (ta.cha.nun) for mercy.' Dan 9:17,18,20,23 — the great Daniel prayer uses ta.cha.nun repeatedly. Closely related to H8467 te.chin.nah (same root, cha.nan). The earnest pleading for mercy vocabulary

operations.description: H6293 pa.ga → extracted. TO INTERCEDE (one sense). 46 occurrences, 43 verses. WARNING: pa.ga has three distinct sen

set.status_note: TO INTERCEDE (one sense). 46 occurrences, 43 verses. WARNING: pa.ga has three distinct senses in the OT: (1) to meet/encounter (Gen 23:8; 28:11; 32:1 — meeting on a road), (2) to fall upon/attack (Judg 8:21; 1Sa 22:17 — military violence), (3) to intercede/entreat on behalf of another (Isa 53:12 — 'made intercession for the transgressors'; Jer 7:16; 27:18; 36:25; Ruth 1:16). The intercession sense

The noun form of H7878 si.ach: the complaint/

set.status_note: MEDITATION (noun form, feminine). 3 occurrences, 3 verses. Job 15:4 — 'you are doing away with the fear of God and hindering meditation (si.chah) before God.' Psa 119:97 — 'oh how I love your law! It is my meditation (si.chah) all the day.' Psa 119:99 — 'I have more understanding... because your testimonies are my meditation (si.chah).' operations.description: H4862 mish.a.lah → extracted. PETITION/DESIRE (noun). 2 occurrences, 2 verses. Psa 20:5 — 'may the Lord fulfil all your

set.status_note: PETITION/DESIRE (noun). 2 occurrences, 2 verses. Psa 20:5 — 'may the Lord fulfil all your petitions (mish.a.lot).' Psa 37:4 — 'he will give you the desires (mish.a.lot) of your heart.' Cross-registry with desire (reg 43) — already noted there. Prayer as the articulated desire brought before God.

operations.description: H1156 be.a → extracted. TO ASK/REQUEST (Aramaic). 12 occurrences, 12 verses. All in Daniel. Dan 6:7,11,12,13 — Dan

set.status_note: TO ASK/REQUEST (Aramaic). 12 occurrences, 12 verses. All in Daniel. Dan 6:7,11,12,13 — Daniel's three-daily prayer practice. Dan 6:10 — 'he went to his house... and prayed (ba.a) and gave thanks before his God, as he had done previously.' The Aramaic prayer verb — Daniel's habitual prayer practice is described with be.a. Cross-registry with desire (reg 43).

operations.description: H7596 she.e.lah → extracted. PETITION/REQUEST (noun). 14 occurrences, 14 verses. 1Sa 1:17 — 'the God of Israel grant yo

Est 5:6,7,8; 7:2,3; 9:12 — Esther's petition to the king. Ezr 7:6 — 'the king granted him all that he asked (she.e.lah).' Hannah's prayer is the paradigm: the petition specifically asked, and s

operations.description: H1159 ba.u → extracted. PETITION (Aramaic noun). 2 occurrences, 2 verses. Dan 6:7 — 'whoever makes petition (ba.u)

set.status_note: PETITION (Aramaic noun). 2 occurrences, 2 verses. Dan 6:7 — 'whoever makes petition (ba.u) to any god or man for thirty days.' Dan 6:13 — 'Daniel pays no attention... and makes his petition (ba.u) three times a day.' The Aramaic noun form of H1156 be.a — Daniel's prayer specifically named as petition/ba.u in the royal decree context.

operations.description: G2171 euchē [vow/prayer] → extracted_thin. 76 occurrences but only 3 verses in export. Act 18:18 — Paul under a vow. Act 21:23 — four men under a vow. Jam 5:15 — 'the prayer of faith will save the sick.' The euchē vocabulary covers the vow-dimension of prayer (the committed prayer) and the faith-prayer of Jam 5:15. Thin extraction: 3 verses insufficient for pattern analysis; requires Phase 2 supplementary verse check.

set.status_note: Vow/prayer vocabulary. Only 3 verses in export despite 76 occurrences — STEP verse coverage issue. Jam 5:15 ('the prayer of faith'), Act 18:18, 21:23 (Paul's vow). Phase 2 verse data check needed.

operations.description: H0577 a.n.na [Please! / I beseech thee] → extracted_thin (rescued from candidate_delete). 13 occurrences, 12 verses. The OT's most urgent entreaty particle — used in prayer contexts where the petitioner is at their most desperate. Gen 50:17 — Joseph's brothers: 'please (a.n.na) forgive the transgression.' Exo 32:31 — Moses: 'alas (a.n.na), this people has sinned.' Neh 1:5 — Nehemiah's prayer opening: 'O Lord God of heaven, the great and awesome God... a.n.na.' Psa 116:4 — 'O Lord, I pray, deliver my soul.' Jon 1:14 — the sailors: 'please (a.n.na), O Lord.' Dan 9:4 — Daniel's prayer. The urgency particle that marks a prayer as a cry from extremity.

set.status_note: Urgency entreaty particle in prayer contexts. Exo 32:31 (Moses), Neh 1:5 (Nehemiah), Psa 116:4, Jon 1:14 (sailors), Dan 9:4 (Daniel). Marks prayer at its most desperate/urgent. Not a prayer verb but the particle that intensifies prayer.

operations.description: Bulk set None-status bleed terms → delete. Three main bleed families: (1) H1697 da.var word sub-glosses (entered via proximity of 'word' to prayer texts — not prayer vocabulary). (2) G3844 para, G2564 kaleō — Greek grammatical/calling bleed. (3) Sickness/weakness vocabulary (H2470/H2483/H4245 — grief/desire registry vocabulary). (4) Asking vocabulary (G3870, G2065, H7592) — these are primary in the desire registry (reg 43) and will be CRL-linked rather than re-extracted here. (5) H4994 na (please particle) — purely grammatical, 260 verses; its urgency dimension is captured by H0577 a.n.na already extracted. (6) Miscellaneous bleed: sanctuary, bee, pestilence, Sheol, love, pasture, raft.

Confirm candidate_delete → delete for remaining candidate_delete terms (all except G1189 deomai, G2172 euchomai, H6739 tse.la, H0577 a.n.na — handled above). Key confirmations: (1) elenchos/elenchō/elegxis/exelenchō/elenchos rebuke family (G1649-1651, G1827, G1246) — no prayer content, entered via 'hearing' or 'speaking' proximity. (2) legō:name (G3004H) — speech act not prayer. (3) e.mer/im.rah/o.mer/o.mer (H0561, H0562, H0565A, H0565B, H0534) — word/saying vocabulary, bleed. (4) pa.lil/pe.li.li/pe.li.liy.yah (H6414, H6416, H6417) — judicial 'judge' vocabulary, entered via H6419 pa.lal proximity (same root but different semantic field). (5) tsa.la/tse.la:limp (H6760, H6761) — entered via H6739 tse.la proximity but different word entirely. (6) pe.ga:chance/miph.ga:target (H6294, H4645) — entered via H6293 pa.ga proximity. (7) cha.nan.el tower (H2606), chin beauty (H2433), cha.ni.nah favour (H2594) — minor bleed. (8) ma.a.mar command (H3982), antilegō/prolegō (G0483, G4302), epilegō/katalegō (G1951, G2639) — speaking/saying vocabulary.

Add cross-registry links. The pray registry has no CRL entries in this export. Required links: (1) desire (reg 43) — the asking vocabulary (sha.al, aiteō, erōtaō, parakaleō) in the desire registry covers the petition/desire dimension of prayer; these are SEMANTIC_OVERLAP, not re-extracted here. (2) spirit (reg 184) — Rom 8:26-27 (Spirit intercedes), 1Co 14:14-15 (spirit prays), Eph 6:18 / Jude 20 (praying in the Spirit) — THEOLOGICAL cross-registry. (3) humility (reg 80) — the posture of prayer (kneeling, face to ground) connects to the humility vocabulary. (4) hope (reg 78) — 'pray without ceasing' / 'devoted to prayer' as expressions of hope-orientation. (5) grief (reg 71) — lament prayer (si.ach complaint vocabulary); parakaleō comfort connection.

links.note: Asking/petition vocabulary primary in desire registry; pray registry focuses on the specifically God-directed prayer vocabulary
Rom 8:26-27 Spirit intercedes; 1Co 14:14-15 my spirit prays; Eph 6:18 / Jude 20 praying in the Spirit
Prayer posture vocabulary shares with humility registry; cha.nan imploring as expression of humility before God
Lament prayer (si.ach as complaint/outpouring) connects grief and prayer; parakaleō bridges petition and comfort

operations.description: FLAG: Four terms are missing from the registry entirely and require a Phase 1 supplement extraction from Claude Code before Session B closure. (1) G1793 entugchanō — to intercede (~5 occ): Rom 8:27 (Spirit intercedes according to the will of God), Rom 8:34 (Christ intercedes at God's right hand), Heb 7:25 (always lives to intercede), Rom 11:2 (Elijah intercedes against Israel), Isa 59:16 LXX. The intercession vocabulary is essential for the Spirit's and Christ's prayer ministry. (2) G1783 enteuxis — intercession noun (~2 occ): 1Ti 2:1 (intercessions for all people), 1Ti 4:5 (sanctified by the word of God and prayer/enteuxis). (3) G1100 glōssa — tongue (~50 occ): 1Co 12:10,28,30; 13:1,8; 14:2-40 — the tongues-prayer vocabulary. 1Co 14:14-15 (my spirit prays in a tongue) is the most direct biblical statement about spirit-to-Spirit prayer. (4) H7836 sha.char — to seek earnestly/at dawn (~12 occ): Psa 63:1 ('O God, you are my God; earnestly I seek you; my soul thirsts for you'), Pro 8:17 — the dawn-prayer seeking vocabulary. These four are absent from the current export and cannot be patched without Claude Code running a Phase 1 supplement.

PH2-212-002: Phase 1 supplement required for G1793, G1783, G1100, H7836 before Session B closure
PH2-212-003: G2171 euchē occ=76 but only 3 verses in export — STEP verse coverage issue
PH2-212-004: H2603A cha.nan is a SHARED_TERM between pray registry and future favour/grace registry
summary.reading_estimate_post_patch: ~553 verses across ~22 extracted terms (before Phase 1 supplement)
